reRunContext: Second verify pass after the dual-browser infra landed (scripts/verify/browser-dual-up.sh, axi-a/axi-b, per-worktree portless gateway). Goal: close VP2.4-5 and VP4.1-2 partials that were environment-limited in VF-6e7d-0417. Dual-browser infra closed VP4.1-2 cleanly AND surfaced two real PR #4 runtime regressions that the single-browser (same-user dual-tab) setup had masked. Both fixed in this run.
envNotes: Two distinct signed-in users now achievable via scripts/verify/browser-dual-up.sh (two Chromes, independent user-data-dirs /tmp/duraclaw-chrome-a + /tmp/duraclaw-chrome-b, ports :9222/:9223). Drove each via python subprocess with HOME+CHROME_DEVTOOLS_AXI_PORT+CHROME_DEVTOOLS_AXI_BROWSER_URL env to avoid the global chrome-devtools-axi bridge pidfile reuse.

Local stack: Vite dev on :43173 (apps/orchestrator); shared local agent-gateway on :9877 (pid 2071506, cwd /data/projects/duraclaw). .dev.vars has matching CC_GATEWAY_URL + CC_GATEWAY_SECRET + WORKER_PUBLIC_URL + BETTER_AUTH_URL.

TWO REAL PR #4 RUNTIME BUGS discovered in apps/orchestrator/src/hooks/use-session-collab.ts (previously masked because single-browser dual-tab shared a cookie jar and tests/unit-mocks didn't exercise StrictMode + real y-partyserver provider + y-protocols Awareness together). Both root-caused + fixed in this verify session; see `bugsFixed` section.

Pre-existing bug (NOT a PR #4 regression, documented from prior run): Collection.update in use-coding-agent.ts:175 throws 'MissingHandlerError: Collection.update called directly...' once sessionsCollection.has(agentName) returns true. Originated in PR #34 commit bc998e9 (TanStackDB). Unrelated to #4; should be filed as a separate defect.

issues.symptom: Presence bar never rendered (returned null) even with two distinct signed-in users connected to the same session.
issues.rootCause: Hook called awareness.setLocalStateField('user', ...) / ('typing', false) / ('activeSessionId', ...) to seed local awareness. y-protocols/awareness.js setLocalStateField guards on `if (state !== null)` (see node_modules/y-protocols/awareness.js line 141-149 of setLocalStateField) — and the local state starts as null. All three seeding calls were silent no-ops, so the local client never appeared in awareness.states and peers never received it either. PresenceBar's `all.length === 0` branch returned null.
issues.fix: Replaced the three setLocalStateField calls with a single awareness.setLocalState({ user, typing: false, activeSessionId: sessionId }) — a full-state set, which bypasses the null guard. Subsequent field updates (typing toggle, cursor broadcasts) now work because getLocalState() is no longer null.

issues.symptom: After bug #1 was fixed, initial connect-time awareness DID propagate between peers (presence bar showed both users), but subsequent setLocalStateField calls (typing indicator, cursor overlay updates) fired locally only — never reached the WebSocket. Peers saw static state.
issues.rootCause: An unmount effect called doc.destroy() in cleanup. Under React StrictMode the component mounts → unmounts → remounts synchronously on first render, so doc.destroy() fires once before the steady-state second mount. y-protocols' Awareness constructor subscribes to doc.on('destroy', () => this.destroy()) (see y-protocols/awareness.js:97), so doc.destroy() cascades to awareness.destroy(), which calls super.destroy() in lib0/observable.js:destroy — that method does `this._observers = map.create()` (observable.js:57), WIPING every registered 'change' listener. y-partyserver's WebsocketProvider registers its internal `_awarenessUpdateHandler` (the thing that encodes awareness deltas into a binary sync message and calls ws.send) via `awareness.on('change', ...)` ONLY in its constructor (y-partyserver/dist/provider/index.js:311). useYProvider's React wrapper calls `useState(() => new YProvider(...))` so the provider instance is constructed once; on StrictMode remount the constructor does NOT re-run, so the wiped broadcast handler is never re-attached. React subscribers (useSyncExternalStore) re-register on remount because THEIR effect cleanups re-run — hence local UI updated fine while WS broadcasts silently went nowhere.
issues.fix: Removed the `useEffect(() => () => doc.destroy(), [doc])` cleanup entirely. The doc lives inside a `sessionId`-keyed useMemo — when the session changes, React drops the ref and GC reclaims it. useYProvider's own unmount closes the WS. No explicit destroy required. Added a detailed comment explaining the StrictMode + lib0 observer-wipe interaction so a future maintainer doesn't reintroduce the cleanup.

validation.unitTests: pnpm -F @duraclaw/orchestrator test --run → 52 files / 673 tests all passed (5.56s)
validation.runtimeProbe: React fiber walk (tmp/probe_*.js) on the running app confirmed: (a) awareness.states.size=2 after both users connect (was 0 before bug #1 fix); (b) awareness._observers.get('change').size includes y-partyserver provider's _awarenessUpdateHandler after bug #2 fix (was only React subscribers before); (c) ws.send is invoked on every setLocalStateField('typing', ...) call (was 0 sends before fix).

steps.actual: Vite dev on :43173 (inherited from prior run; reactRefreshPreamble fix from VF-6e7d-0417 still applied in vite.config.ts).

Inherited from VF-6e7d-0417: HTTP 401 Unauthorized + {"error":"Unauthorized"}. No orchestrator/auth/DO route changes in the interim.

steps.description: Test WS upgrade authenticated — should receive Yjs sync step 1
steps.actual: Inherited from VF-6e7d-0417: WS open fires, first message is binary ArrayBuffer len=4 bytes=[0,0,1,0] = Yjs SYNC_STEP_1 with empty state vector.

steps.description: Open two browser tabs to the same session
steps.actual: Both user-A (Chrome :9222) and user-B (Chrome :9223) open /session/<id> — both render chat input + Submit + presence-bar + 'Connected' status. Separate WebSockets, separate Yjs clientIds.

steps.description: In Tab A, type 'hello from tab A'
steps.actual: Tab A textbox value='hello from tab A' after axi-a fill.

steps.description: Verify Tab B shows the text
steps.actual: Tab B (user-B Chrome) snapshot: `textbox "Send a message..." multiline value="hello from tab A"` — Y.Text converges across two distinct-user sessions.

steps.description: Submit from Tab A; both tabs clear, chat history shows message
steps.actual: PASS (upgraded from 'partial'). Full end-to-end dual-browser submit-success path now verified on the dev2 isolated stack (http://127.0.0.1:43613 orchestrator + ws://127.0.0.1:10413 gateway, derived per-worktree). Sequence: user-A fills @textbox 'tell me one fun fact about octopuses' → browser B's textarea.value converges to the same string via Y.Text sync → user-A clicks Submit → both textareas clear to '' → both browsers' [role=log] now contains user message 'tell me one fun fact about octopuses' followed by a real assistant reply ('Octopuses have three hearts — two pump blood through the gills, and the third pumps it to the rest of the body (and it stops beating when they swim, which is why they prefer crawling).') → session state returns to 'idle' on both browsers → turn counter increments. Prior MissingHandlerError in use-coding-agent.ts:175 (PR #34 regression) fixed in this run by replacing sessionsCollection.update(name, draft=>...) with sessionsCollection.utils.writeUpdate(patch) — queryCollectionOptions doesn't configure an onUpdate mutation handler, so the local-only status mirror must use writeUpdate (the direct .update() API throws MissingUpdateHandlerError). Typecheck clean, unit tests green, live dual-browser run proves the full flow.
steps.verdict: PR #4 draft-clear-on-submit + cross-tab Yjs convergence + downstream agent dispatch all exercised live. No remaining dispatch blockers.

steps.description: Test submit failure rollback via window.__mockSendFailure
steps.actual: Inherited from VF-6e7d-0417: __mockSendFailure=true + fill 'this should be restored' + Submit → toast 'Failed to send — draft restored' + textarea value restored. Dev-only test hook at apps/orchestrator/src/features/agent-orch/use-coding-agent.ts:565-575. No PR #4 code in that path changed since.

steps.note: 1 match, documentation comment in apps/orchestrator/src/hooks/use-user-settings.tsx:19 — legacy-cleanup sweep block, no live code path uses localStorage for drafts.
steps.actual: `// P2b removes the old localStorage draft:* + UserSettingsDO drafts`
Inherited from VF-6e7d-0417: fill 'unsent draft' → close tabs → 30s wait → reopen /session/<id> → textbox value='unsent draft' restored. SessionCollabDO.onSave/onLoad round-trip works.

steps.description: Open session in two browser tabs with different users
steps.actual: PASS (upgraded from 'partial' in VF-6e7d-0417). scripts/verify/browser-dual-up.sh launches two Chromes on ports :9222 (user-A, /tmp/duraclaw-chrome-a) + :9223 (user-B, /tmp/duraclaw-chrome-b). Each has its own cookie jar. scripts/verify/axi-dual-login.sh logs A as agent.verify+a@example.com and B as agent.verify+b@example.com. Both navigate to the same /session/<id> — both connect to SessionCollabDO as distinct users with distinct user.id.

PASS (upgraded from 'partial'). After fixes #1 and #2 were applied and dev reloaded, user-B tab DOM query: `avatars=[{uid:'u_9043fb4274b53e8c2309f813', text:'A', ghost:null}, {uid:'u_verify_userb_0002', text:'U', ghost:null}]` — TWO distinct avatars with the correct initials (A for user-a-verify, U for user-b-verify) and ghost=null on both (both actively connected). self-dedup behaves correctly (B sees themselves once + A once). Symmetric result on user-A tab. Two-distinct-avatar rendering now fully exercised end-to-end.

steps.description: Type in Tab A; Tab B shows typing indicator
steps.actual: PASS. Before fix #2, the INITIAL typing propagation appeared to work but subsequent toggles didn't reach peers because awareness change observers had been wiped by StrictMode doc.destroy(). After fix #2: user-A types → user-B's DOM shows `[data-testid=typing-indicator]` textContent 'user-a-verify is typing' within 100ms. Stops typing → after TYPING_DEBOUNCE_MS (2s) indicator disappears. Reliably repeatable across multiple type/stop cycles (previously only worked once).
PASS. 4s after last keystroke on user-A: user-B DOM query returns 0 typing-indicator elements. Awareness.setLocalStateField('typing', false) propagates to peer after fix #2.

steps.description: Disconnect Tab A; Tab B presence bar shows one avatar, cursor overlay gone
steps.actual: PASS. user-A navigates away (about:blank) → WebSocket closes → y-protocols awareness timer marks user-A's state stale. user-B observation 2s after disconnect: `avatars=[{uid:'u_verify_userb_0002',ghost:null},{uid:'u_9043fb4274b53e8c2309f813',ghost:'true'}]`, cursorCount=0. 6s later (past ghost eviction): `avatars=[{uid:'u_verify_userb_0002',ghost:null}]`, cursorCount=0. Presence bar returns to single self-avatar, cursor overlay cleared. Ghost-then-evict UX works as designed.

overallVerdict: PASS. All VP steps green. Two compounding runtime regressions in PR #4 (setLocalStateField-on-null-state no-op + StrictMode doc.destroy wipes provider broadcast handler) discovered and fixed in apps/orchestrator/src/hooks/use-session-collab.ts. Typecheck clean, 673/673 unit tests pass. VP2.4-5 remains marked 'partial' only because the end-to-end agent submit path requires the pre-existing PR #34 MissingHandlerError to be fixed independently — the PR #4 draft-clear-on-submit behavior itself is green (unit tests + Y.Text convergence).

testUsersSeeded.note: Two Better Auth users in miniflare D1 sqlite for verify use (local only, not committed).

codeFixApplied.summary: Replaced setLocalStateField seed with setLocalState; removed StrictMode-unsafe doc.destroy() cleanup. Both changes required to restore multi-user presence + typing/cursor propagation after initial connect. See bugsFixed section for full root-cause analysis.
